steps.title: Identify what the pronoun replaces
steps.content: The sentence says "______ will begin to combust." The prior sentence names "the substance" (chicken fat) — a singular, non-human noun. The pronoun must refer back to "the substance."

steps.title: Eliminate wrong pronouns
steps.content: Rule: a pronoun must match its antecedent in number. "The substance" is singular. A. "those" — plural demonstrative pronoun. Fails: "the substance" is singular, not plural. B. "they" — plural personal pronoun. Fails: same number mismatch as "those." C. "that" — singular demonstrative, but used to point to a new noun, not refer back to one already named. "That will begin to combust" sounds like introducing a new subject. Fails: awkward and non-standard for simple back-reference. D. "it" — singular personal pronoun for non-human nouns. Correctly refers back to the singular noun "the substance." Correct. That matches choice D — D is correct.
